cot_prefix: Okay, let's solve this logic problem step by step. We have 5 fruits: apples, oranges, watermelons, pears, and mangoes. We need to determine their order of prices from most expensive to least expensive. Given information: 1. Apples are more expensive than oranges. (A > O) 2. Oranges are more expensive than mangoes. (O > M) 3. Apples are the third-most expensive. 4. Pears are more expensive than watermelons. (P > W) Let me try to determine the order. From (1) and (2), we have A > O > M From (3), apples are third-most expensive. That means there are exactly two fruits more expensive than apples. Let me try to determine the full
